title: The One Where Ross Dates A Student

lines.text: Monica and Chandler's, Chandler, Rachel, and Joey are there as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! I just got uh, my teacher evaluations! Check out what this one student wrote, "I loved Dr. Geller's class. Mind blowing lectures! Dr. Geller, you are definitely the hottie of the paleontology department!"

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahh, Hotties of the Paleontology Department, there's a big selling calendar, eh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who wrote it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I wish I knew, but the evaluations are all anonymous.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey, do you still have their final exams?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, 'cause you can just match the evaluation to the exam with the same handwriting and boom, there's your admirer. (Ross is stunned.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A hot girl's at stake and all of the sudden he's Rain Man.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, wait a minute, wait a minute, why are we so sure that this is a girl?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's a girl! Anyway, it wou-it wouldn't matter. Okay? Because I'm a teacher and she's a student.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, is that against the rules?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, but it is frowned upon.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Besides, there's a big age difference.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, well think of it like this, when you're 90...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know when I'm 90 she'll be like 80 and it won't seem like such a big difference.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No that's not what I was going to say at all. No, what I was going to say is when you're 90 you'll still have the memory of what it was like to be with a 20-year-old.
Opening Credits
Monica and Chandler's, Phoebe, Monica, and Chandler are eating breakfast as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Chandler! Y'know that girl you went to college with who-who became a movie director?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, Dana Keystone. She was in my Movement class.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What's a Movement class?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's Chandler's way of pretending he didn't take mime.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well listen, anyway she's directing the new Al Pacino movie. You gotta get me an audition!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I don't know man. I haven't talked to her in like ten years.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no, please-please Chandler I-I-I would owe you so much!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You do owe me so much. You owe me three thousand, four hundred...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey dude, why are you changing the subject? Why? Will you make the call or what?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh okay, I'll-I'll try.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! Thanks! You're the best! Now listen, the last day of auditions is Thursday. Okay? So I gotta get in there by Thursday. Okay? Just remember Thursday. Thursday. Can you remember Thursday?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah so, Tuesday?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (angrily) Thursday! Look if you need help remembering think of like this, the third day. All right? Monday, one day. Tuesday, two day. Wednesday, when? Huh? What day? Thursday! The third day! Okay?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sarcastically) Thank you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering from the bathroom) Hey Mon? I'm gonna check my messages.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You just thought of that in there?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah sure, nature called, she wanted to see who else did.
Rachel dials her number.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on phone) Hello? (Shocked that someone answered.) Uh, Rachel. (To the gang.) Great, someone is in our apartment. Call the cops!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're on the phone!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on phone) Oh my God! Oh my God! Thank you! (Hangs up) That was the fire department, there was a fire at our place!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! How bad was it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, he didn't say, but it was a fire. I'm guessing not very good. Come on, we gotta go!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on! (The girls all run out and Joey holds up Chandler by smiling.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you smiling about? What is so funny?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The part I want to audition for is a fireman, this is so meant to be!
N.Y.U. Paleontology department, Ross is putting up the grades on the message board.

lines.speaker: Burt
lines.text: (another professor) Wow! It looks like you were very generous with your grades this semester! (Ross frantically starts to change some as a female student, Elizabeth, approaches.)

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Professor Geller?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (startled) Yeah?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: I wanted to say how much I enjoyed your class.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh thank you. Thanks very much. (Leans up against the board and on a thumbtack.) Ow! (He pulls away.)

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Uh, I'm a little embarrassed about calling you a hottie on my evaluation...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That was you?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Yeah. I felt a little weird about it. You're a teacher. I'm a student. But would you maybe want to go out with me sometime?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh I, I don't-I don't think that would be the best idea.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Oh, because I was thinking, the semester's over; you're not my teacher anymore.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What time?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: (checks her grade) Oh, y'know what? Forget it, you already gave me an A.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Gotcha. (Starts to leave.)

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: I'm kidding!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! So-so seriously, what time?
Phoebe and Rachel's, everyone minus Ross are arriving to inspect the damage. The fire department is still there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Coming through! (Has to dodge a fireman) Oh! Coming through! (Sees a cute one.) Oh! Hello! Hi! (Smiles then realizes) No! Right! Coming through!
They start to look around seeing that the living room is undamaged.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh well, it's not so bad.
Fireman #1: Yeah, most of the damage is pretty mostly contained in the bedrooms.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My God! (They both run into the bedrooms.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to the fireman) Hey buddy, do you think I can borrow your uniform this Thursday?
Fireman #1: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey! He's working! (To Chandler) You would look good in that.
Phoebe and Rachel return

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, how bad is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's bad. It's really bad. The only thing in there that isn't burned is an ass. Which I do not remember buying!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How's your room Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Everything's ruined. My bed. My clothes. Look at my favorite blue sweater. (Hold it up.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Isn't that mine?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Fine! I'm sorry for your loss! (Hands it back to her.)
Fireman #1: So uh, you're not gonna be able to live here for a while, you ladies have a place to stay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (stands up) Wow! Oh-okay, look pal, I am not in the mood to be hit on right now! But if you give me your number I will call you some other time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (pulling Rachel back down) Yes, they can stay with us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Have you figured out what started the fire Mr. Fireman?
Fireman #1: Well uh, do either of you smoke?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, not usually. But yeah, I could use one right now.
Fireman #1: No-no-no, do you uh light candles? Burn incense?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes! I do! All the time! I love them! Oh my God! I did it! It's me! It's me! I burned down the house! I burned down the house!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Phoebe calm down, there's no need to place blame. Okay? (To the fireman) I warned her about those candles.
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is on the phone as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: God, it's great to catch up! I can't believe how long it's been!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler, is that... (Mimes someone using a movie camera and Chandler nods yes.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (on phone) Oh that's great! Good for you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey listen... (Chandler waves him away.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! So yeah, maybe we can get together umm... (Joey mimes throwing something in the air, catching it, rolling it out, putting it in an oven and cutting it.) Can you hold for one second please? (To Joey) What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: When you're off the phone, do you wanna get a pizza?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (on phone) Hi! I'm back. (Goes to hang up the phone.) Yeah, that sounds great. (Listens) Okay. Well, we'll do it then. (Listens) Okay, bye-bye. (He hangs up the phone and turns around to see Joey standing close to him and screams.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey listen, so when's-when's my audition? I mean I know it's Thursday, but what time?
Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Joey) We didn't get to the audition. I'm gonna take her to coffee and then we'll do it then.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah-ha!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! So, now you're going on a date with this girl?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, it is not a date! I haven't talked to her in ten years! You can't just call up somebody you haven't talked to in ten years and ask them for a favor. There are rules, y'know? You gotta, you got to put in some time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're right, I'm sorry. It's not like you're y'know, going out with an ex-girlfriend.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, we only went out once.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You dated her!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not once!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, well why don't I go out with an ex-boyfriend and do Joey a favor?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, you wanna do Joey a favor, maybe you go out with Joey. (He turns around to see Chandler glaring at him.)
Central Perk, Chandler is having coffee with Dana.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (laughs) Oh that's great, my friend Joey's in the movie business.

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Y'know who I ran into from school? Howie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (giggles) My friend's name is Joey.

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Apparently Howie's editing now. Yeah, he-he-he calls me up and asks me if he can edit my new movie. Can you believe that?! Y'know I-I-I haven't spoken to him in like ten years and he asks me for a favor!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I've always hated that Howie.

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: No, no "How you doing?" Man, I mean not even a cup of coffee first!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The nerve huh?

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Refill?
Monica and Chandler's, Monica is about to debut Hotel Monica (formerly known as Rachel's Room) to Phoebe and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now, this is last minute so I want to apologize for the mess. Okay?
They walk into a completely redecorated and repainted room. And of course, the room is immaculate. Only an obsessive-compulsive like Monica could find fault with the room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! It sure didn't look this way when I lived here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know! Now look, there's only one problem though. There's only room for one, so I guess one of you will have to stay at Joey's.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, since the fire was kinda my fault I guess (To Rachel) you should get to stay here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Hey-hey, now this was no one's fault Pheebs. Okay? It was an accident.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well no, it was my fault so you should get the nice room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! (She jumps on the bed.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So Rach! You're the first guest at Hotel Monica! Umm, you'll just have to tell me how you like your eggs in the morning. And I thought I would bring them to you, y'know, in bed. Oh, you have been through so much.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I have.
Joey's apartment, Joey is showing Phoebe around.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This right here is where I keep the pizza. (He points to the chair.) And uh that's where the napkin is. (Points to the floor next to the chair.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What's that smell?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know! (Shrugs his shoulders.)
A street, Ross is walking with Elizabeth on their date.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Oh please! It was such a big class! You never even noticed me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Of course I did! You uh, you sat next to Sleepy Sleeperson.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh uh, I had trouble remembering everyone's name, so I-I kinda came up with nicknames. Like the guy on the other side of you was Smelly von Brownshirt.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Oh yeah. So umm, did you have a nickname for me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, no. No.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Yes you did! What was it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, it's Cutie McPretty.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Ohh that's so sweet!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen, I gotta tell ya, I-I'm having a great time! Y'know how before you said it might be weird, the whole student teacher thing, and to be frank I thought it would be too, but it's not. I mean it's not at all.

lines.speaker: Burt
lines.text: (exiting from a restaurant) Dr. Geller!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (pushing Elizabeth into a doorway) Burt!
Phoebe and Rachel's, Phoebe and Rachel are cleaning up the mess.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So did you sleep well last night?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I did.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I did, Monica was so sweet she left a little mint on my pillow.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know what Joey left on my pillow?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Gum!
Fireman #2: Well, we determined the cause of the fire.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah I know, it was my candle. My candle!
Fireman #2: No, there was an appliance left on in the bathroom. It's looks like a curling iron.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't use a curling iron.
Fireman #2: Well someone does.
Phoebe looks at Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, don't look at me! My hair's straight! Straight! Straight! Straight!
Fireman #2: It could've been a hair straightener.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.
Joey's apartment, Joey is now showing Rachel around.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is where I keep the pizza. (It's the same location as before.) And-Hey! Where did the napkin go?! (The napkin is not in it's spot.)
Commercial Break
Monica and Chandler's, Phoebe is reading and Monica runs over and turns on the lamp behind Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now there you go! I wouldn't want my best guest to strain her eyes!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thanks Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Does that smell bother you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What the smell from Joey's? No, I can hardly smell it over here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well you let me know if you can, because y'know I can bake a pie to cover it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can smell it a little, bake the pie.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.
The phone rings and Monica answers it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello? (Listens) Oh yes, one minute please. (To Phoebe) It's for you. It's the fire inspector.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! (Takes the phone.) Hello? (Listens) Yeah this is Phoebe. (Listens) Really?!
[Cut to Joey's, he's on the phone.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (in a funny voice) Yeah, so it turns out that it wasn't the hair straightener that started the fire. (Rachel prompts him on what to say next.) No-no, it was the candles. It's very not good leaving candles unattended. In fact, one of the first things they teach you in fire school is... (Phoebe suddenly enters.) Uhh... Uhh... Okay. Well, I have to go now. (Phoebe leaves.)
Joey's apartment, Ross is telling Monica and Joey about his date with Elizabeth.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We had such a great time! She's-she's incredible! I thought the-the age difference might be a problem, but it wasn't. It wasn't at all. Elizabeth is very mature for her age. (Joey makes the international sign for big boobies.) (To Monica) A concept lost on some people!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So it's okay to date a student.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, not really. I mean technically it's-it's not against the rules or anything, but it is frowned upon. Especially by that professor we ran into last night, Judgey von Holierthanthou.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well Ross, you be careful now. You don't want to get a reputation as y'know Professor McNailshisstudents.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. What-what should I do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well Ross, it seems pretty clear. I mean what's more important? What people think or how you feel, huh? Ross, you gotta follow your heart.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey that is so sweet.
He turns his back to Monica and does the international sign for big boobies again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey! So, how did it go with Dana? Any reason I should leave a block of time open say Thursday?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I couldn't do it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You couldn't do it?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, relax I just need more time. We're going to dinner tonight.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! You're going out with her again!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Going out with who?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, Dana Keystone from college.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah! Wasn't she uh... (Does the international sign for big boobies.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, that was Dana Caplin.

lines.speaker: The Guys
lines.text: Ohhhh! (They all look up in a moment of reflection.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey! You didn't even know her!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah whatever!
A Restaurant, Chandler is having dinner with Dana.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Great story again! The yarns that you weave! Woo-hoo-hoo!

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Y'know uh, actually I-I-I should get going.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no! Stay! Stay! Because you-you should you-you-you should stay!

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Wow. Oh, I am really flattered, but I just I don't feel that way about you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, that's not-No-no-no!

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: I'm sorry Chandler, y'know you are such a sweet guy and I, I don't want to hurt you. Oh, I wish there was something I can do to make you feel better.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-Really?!

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (breaking up) Well, it just hurts so bad. I uh...

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Ohh, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well maybe there is one thing you can do.

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: What?! Anything! Anything!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I umm, I mean this is just off the top of my head now, umm but I have this friend. This actor friend and he would kill me if he thought I was doing this umm, but umm would it be possible for him to get an audition for your movie say on Thursday?

lines.speaker: Dana
lines.text: Absolutely! But you-you would really feel better about me rejecting you if your actor friend can audition for my movie?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, the heart wants what it wants. I'll see you later. (He runs off and leaves her with the bill, which the waiter promptly delivers.)
Hotel Monica, Phoebe is on the bed playing her guitar as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! How's it goin'?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, not much has changed in the last five minutes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes it has! I made cookies!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh that's all right. I'm still full from your homemade potato chips.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But you should eat them now because they're hot from the oven.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Reaches for one.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (pulling the plate back) Oh-ho! But not in here! Can't eat 'em in bed, remember? No crumbies!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (gritting her teeth) Okay, I'll be out in a second.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!
Monica leaves and Phoebe closes the door behind her and tries to lock it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (opening the door) What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That doesn't lock does it?
Joey's apartment, Joey and Rachel are eating spaghetti in the living room while watching TV and Rachel drops some on the floor.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Joey! Sorry!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No that's all right. Don't worry about it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh but look! That's gonna leave a stain!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach! Hey! It's fine! You're at Joey's!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Look! (He throws some of his spaghetti on the floor.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I've never lived like this before.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know.
Rachel throws some of hers down.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, don't waste it, I mean its still food. (He picks it up and eats it.)
Central Perk, Ross and Elizabeth are deciding what to do on their second date.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What do you want to do now? Huh? You wanna go get a drink?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Uhh, I can't.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you have some studying to do?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: No, I have some turning 21 to do.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know I remember when uh, when I was in college, we used to... (He sees some of his colleagues enter and puts his head on her lap.)

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nothing, I'm-I'm just, I'm so comfortable with you!

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Do you not want to be seen with me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? No! Of course, of course I do! Are-are they gone?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Uh no, they're still here but I think I'm about to leave.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! No! No! Wait! You're right, this is stupid. Who cares what people think? I mean, I mean we like each other right? There's nothing wrong with that. Come on. (They get up and go over to the table where his colleagues are sitting.) Burt? Lydia? Mel? This is Elizabeth.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Lydia
lines.text: Aren't you in my Popular Culture class?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right Lydia, Elizabeth here is a student and uh, we're dating. And you may frown upon that, but we're not gonna hide it anymore.

lines.speaker: Mel
lines.text: You are so fired.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Burt
lines.text: They're gonna fire you! You can't date a student! It's against the rules.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really? Its not just frowned upon?
Joey's apartment, Joey and Rachel are throwing huge spitballs at the entertainment center.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I love it at Joey's!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, here you go. (Hands her another one.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering, sees Rachel's throw) Ni-hi-ice!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Joey) So, you busy Thursday?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, very funny. I don't know if you remember, but my audition was supposed to be Thursday. (Chandler doesn't say anything until Joey figures it out.) You got me the audition?! Let's hug it up! (They hug.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Okay. (Joey puts him down.) What are you gonna do to me if you get the part?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey! Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Rachel) Listen umm, yeah okay, I need to talk to you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now I know that they said that the umm, the hair straightener started the fire but I think I'm partly at fault. You see, I didn't, I didn't tell you but umm, but I-I had recently refilled the tissues and so y'know let's just face it, that's just kindling! So I think it's better that I stay at Joey's.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no-no-no. Phoebe, this was my fault and besides y'know what? I'm fine here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Oh umm, Chandler, Monica is looking for you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah she said something about crumbies.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! No! No! I was so careful! (Runs out.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Rachel) Okay, you have to switch with me! Monica is driving me crazy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's right, all the ladies want to stay at Joey's.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No! Phoebe, come on! I don't want to switch! Please come on! I can throw wet paper towels here!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No but at Monica's you can eat cookies over the sink!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right fine! This looks like so much fun. (Examining the bowl of wet paper towels.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God, what a mess. (She grabs the bowl and heads for Monica's.)
Ross's apartment, Ross is looking at his handbook. Elizabeth is also there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! It actually is in the handbook. I can't date you or have a hot plate in my office. I can't believe we have to stop seeing each other.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: For what it's worth I did appreciate you standing up for me. It felt really nice. It kinda made me like you even more.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know, I know I really like you too. But we-we can't date. It's against the rules. It's forbidden.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Just hearing you describe it as forbidden, it's really hot.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I-I-I don't care how hot it is it's-it's uh, it's wrong.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Stop it! (She starts to get all worked up.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (feeling it too) No! No! It's wrong! It's-it's-it's naughty. It's taboo.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Shut the book!
They frantically start making out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let's also get a hot plate!
They start making out again.
Ending Credits
Joey's apartment, Joey is eating pizza as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's Thursday! How was the audition?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wh? Monday, one day. Tuesday, two day. Wednesday, when huh what? Thurs-(He gasps in horror.) Oh! (Runs out and Chandler just shakes his head. After a second Joey runs back in to grab a piece of pizza and runs back out.)
End
